items.title: IOS-XE Basic Troubleshooting and Configuration Guide

steps.action: Check device reachability and basic connectivity.
steps.expected: Device responds to ping and show commands; interfaces show line protocol up.
Interface is administratively up/line protocol up with correct IP addressing.

steps.action: Check routing table and static/default routes.
steps.expected: Host routes and/or default route present; next-hop reachable.

steps.action: Verify dynamic routing neighbor adjacencies (if used).
steps.expected: Established neighbor relationships (OSPF/IS-IS/BGP) with correct peer IPs.

steps.action: Check control-plane and management plane health.
steps.expected: CPU/memory within normal ranges; no critical syslog errors.

steps.action: Validate NAT, ACLs, and policy-maps if applied.
steps.expected: NAT translations present/empty as expected; ACL hit counts incrementing correctly.
Running config saved to startup-config; change log updated.

steps.action: Verify Nexus service status and basic connectivity.
steps.expected: Nexus is running and reachable on expected ports.

steps.action: Check Nexus logs for errors or warnings.
steps.expected: No critical errors; logs show normal startup.
Required ports (e.g., 8081, 8443) are open and not blocked.
Repositories are in service and proxying correctly.

steps.action: Check storage and disk space for blob stores.
steps.expected: Sufficient disk space; blob stores are in service.

steps.action: Validate and repair database if needed.
steps.expected: Database is healthy; no corruption detected.

steps.action: Review and update Nexus configuration (security, users, roles).
steps.expected: Security settings align with policy; admin user is accessible.
Nexus restarts cleanly and returns to expected state.
WLC is powered on, LEDs indicate normal operation, and Ethernet link LEDs are active.

steps.action: Access the WLC CLI via console or SSH
steps.expected: Successful login to the WLC command-line interface.
No critical alarms; system uptime and resources are normal.
Management interface has correct IP, gateway, and VLAN settings.

steps.action: Validate AP connectivity and join status
steps.expected: APs are in 'RUN' state and associated with the WLC.
Radio profiles, WLANs, and client settings are correctly configured.
Client can associate to WLAN and obtain IP via DHCP with full connectivity.

steps.action: Review and apply firmware/software updates if needed
steps.expected: WLC runs recommended firmware version with no known bugs.
Running config saved to startup-config and change log updated.

items.title: PAN-OS Basic Troubleshooting and Configuration Guide

steps.action: Check device connectivity and management access.
steps.expected: Management interface is reachable and you can log in via SSH/HTTPS.

steps.action: Verify PAN-OS software version and license status.
steps.expected: Device runs a supported PAN-OS version and license is valid.

steps.action: Check interface status and IP configuration.
steps.expected: Interfaces are administratively up/up with correct IP/subnet.
Routing table contains correct next-hop/default route.
Ping/traceroute to external IP/DNS succeeds.

steps.action: Check security policies and allow traffic.
steps.expected: Policy rules permit intended traffic between zones.

steps.action: Enable logging and verify traffic logs.
steps.expected: Logs show allowed/blocked sessions as expected.
commands: set logging setting filter rule <rule-name> any any any any any no-log disable

steps.action: Check HA status if High Availability is configured.
steps.expected: HA state is active/passive or active/active as configured.
Commit succeeds and changes are active on device(s).

steps.action: Save configuration to ensure persistence across reboot.
steps.expected: Configuration is saved and will survive reboot.
Device is powered on, all LEDs indicate normal operation (no fault LEDs). Management interface has link.

steps.action: Ping the management interface from a known host.
steps.expected: Successful ping responses (latency < 50 ms) confirming IP reachability.

steps.action: Check interface status and IP configuration.
steps.expected: Interfaces are administratively up/up with correct IP/subnet.

steps.action: Verify default route and routing table.
steps.expected: Default route present and active; remote networks appear in routing table.
DNS queries resolve to expected IPs; 'show system dns setting' shows valid servers.
Device shows connected to Panorama with correct group and template stack.
No critical/err entries; security logs show expected traffic.
Policies show incremental hit counts for allowed/blocked traffic.

steps.action: Check threat prevention and decryption settings.
steps.expected: Decryption policies and anti-virus profiles are correctly applied; no unexpected bypass.
Commit succeeds without errors; devices report 'success' post-push.
New/updated policies reflected in 'show session all' and counters increase as expected.

items.title: IOS-XE Basic Connectivity Troubleshooting and Configuration Guide

steps.expected: Verify device is powered, fans and LEDs are normal; interfaces show appropriate link status.

steps.action: Verify physical connectivity and cable integrity
steps.expected: Link/up on both ends for the relevant interface.
Correct IP addressing, subnet, and default gateway; reachable next-hop.
ARP entry present; MAC learned on the correct interface.

steps.action: Confirm DHCP or static IP assignment
steps.expected: Host receives correct IP configuration via DHCP or static config.
No deny ACEs blocking required traffic; inspection policies as intended.
Neighbors established; routes installed.
No critical/emergency errors; relevant informational messages present.

steps.action: Apply minimal config to restore connectivity (if needed)
steps.expected: Interface is administratively up/up with correct IP; traffic flows.
WLC power LED steady, fans running, management interface link up.

steps.action: Access the WLC CLI via console or SSH
steps.expected: Login prompt or command-line interface available.

steps.action: Check WLC software version and health
steps.expected: Version output matches expected release; system status shows healthy.
Management interface has correct IP/subnet; VLANs are defined and active.

steps.action: Check AP reachability and join status
steps.expected: APs are in joined/running state with correct model and config.
WLANs are enabled with correct SSID, security, and VLAN mapping.
RADIUS servers reachable; shared secret matches; auth policies applied.
Clients associated; DHCP pools active; IP assignment working.
Channels non-overlapping; power levels within regulatory domain.

steps.action: Inspect logs for errors and warnings
steps.expected: No critical errors; relevant warnings investigated.
Configuration saved to startup; change record updated.
